title: Job Openings at Mar&Mor Integrated Services Limited
content:
MAR&MOR is a building services company that is passionate about customer satisfaction, best practices and standards compliance. We provide semi-last mile services to improve nearly every aspect of your mechanical and electrical installations. We Design, Procure, install and maintain Heating,Ventilation and Air Conditioning (HVAC) systems, water treatment, Firefighting and water supply systems as well as power supply, electrical network and communication systems to meet industrial, commercial or residential requirements. We also provide quality general plumbing services. Over the years, we have gained outstanding experience delivering quality service to a wide range of discerning clients, gathering a considerable knowledge base working on projects, with a solid team of engineers and technicians with an average of 10 years experiential practice - committed to providing products and services of the highest quality. Today, we offer these resources and experience to help you lower maintenance and repair cost; reduce downtime and improve productivity; increase operating efficiency and achieve a better return on assets. Established in 2008, MAR&MOR continues to grow, with a strong reputation for delivering high quality, fully integrated electrical and mechanical installations. The Key: we have always understood the need to provide better services at competitive prices. We place so much emphasis on continuous process improvement and are poised to reach quality levels that set new standards in the industry. Our primary goal is to deliver best practices and a comfortable environment for our clients.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Procurement Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	As we continue to grow, we are looking for an experienced Procurement Officer to join our team and support the efficient procurement of equipment and materials for our projects.
	If you have a strong background in procurement, supplier management, importation, and logistics coordination, we would love to hear from you.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage end-to-end procurement activities, including sourcing, supplier evaluation, supplier onboarding, and contract negotiations.
	Develop and implement procurement strategies that ensure value for money, transparency, and compliance.
	Coordinate import and shipping processes, liaising with regulatory agencies such as NAFDAC, SON, and Nigeria Customs Service, as well as freight and logistics providers.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with local and international suppliers to ensure quality, competitive pricing, and timely delivery.
	Identify and onboard alternative suppliers to minimize supply risks and support business continuity.
	Prepare and analyze landed cost estimates to support procurement planning and budgeting.
	Monitor procurement budgets, cost-saving initiatives, and procurement performance.
	Collaborate with project and internal teams to deliver procurement solutions that meet operational and project requirements.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and all applicable procurement and supply chain regulations.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in procurement, including hands-on experience managing importation and shipping processes.
	Strong knowledge of supplier sourcing, procurement planning, contract negotiation, and inventory management.
	Proficiency in ERP systems.
	Experience preparing and analyzing landed cost estimates.
	Excellent analytical, negotiation, communication, and organizational skills.
	Ability to thrive in a fast-paced project environment.
	Experience within the building services, MEP, engineering, or construction industry will be an added advantage.


Why Join Mar&Mor?


	At Mar&Mor, you'll be part of a team delivering impactful engineering projects while working in a collaborative, performance-driven environment that values professionalism, innovation, and continuous improvement.


Application Closing Date: 27th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

 

2.) ELV Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Description


	We are seeking an experienced ELV Engineer with proven expertise in the design, installation, testing, commissioning, and maintenance of Extra Low Voltage (ELV) systems.
	The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience across multiple ELV services including CCTV, access control, fire suppression, fire detection and alarm systems, Building Management Systems (BMS), and other related technologies.


Key Responsibilities


	Design, implement, and maintain ELV systems including: CCTV surveillance, Access control, Fire detection and suppression, BMS, and other ELV services.
	Perform site surveys, prepare technical documentation, and support system design and integration.
	Supervise installation, configuration, and commissioning of ELV systems in compliance with project specifications and industry standards.
	Conduct troubleshooting, testing, and preventive maintenance of ELV systems.
	Collaborate with contractors, consultants, and project teams to ensure timely project delivery.
	Ensure compliance with health and safety regulations, codes, and standards.
	Provide technical support, training, and guidance to junior engineers/technicians.
	Maintain accurate project documentation, reports, and as-built drawings.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree / Diploma in Electrical, Electronics, Communications, or related fields.
	Minimum of 3 years of experience working as an ELV Engineer or in a similar role.
	Strong hands-on knowledge of ELV systems including CCTV, access control, fire detection & suppression, and BMS.
	Familiarity with relevant international standards (NFPA, BS, EN, etc.) and local authority regulations.
	Proficient in reading and interpreting technical drawings and schematics.
	Good understanding of networking protocols (IP addressing, TCP/IP) for integrated ELV solutions.
	Excellent troubleshooting and problem-solving skills.
	Strong communication and coordination abilities.
	Ability to work independently and within a team under tight deadlines.


Application Closing Date: 24th July, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

3.) PMO Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary


	We are seeking a PMO Engineer (HVAC) to support mechanical engineering projects by ensuring coordination, quality assurance, and technical compliance throughout project execution.
	The role will ensure mechanical systems are delivered on schedule, within budget, and align with client and industry standards.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop project plans, schedules, and assist with progress monitoring.
	Review electrical drawings and specifications for compliance.
	Ensure quality assurance through inspections and testing.
	Coordinate with MEPF teams to integrate electrical systems seamlessly.
	Address client queries and assist in procurement activities.
	Propose improvements for efficiency and compliance.


Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering or Electrical Engineering
	Experience: Minimum of 5 years in Mechanical Engineering, preferably in MEPF/building services.
	Certifications: COREN or equivalent membership is a plus.


Skills:


	Proficiency in electrical design tools (AutoCAD, Revit MEP).
	Strong understanding of electrical systems and safety regulations.
	Excellent communication, problem-solving, and multitasking skills.


Click here to apply    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Kimeric Nigeria Limited
content:
Kimeric Nigeria Limited is a Company that has acquired a reputation for rendering quality Onshore/Offshore supply and engineering services to the Oil and Gas industry in Nigeria and West Africa sub-region.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Administrative - Control Coordinator / Administrator II

Job Location: Lagos

Main Functions
     

            
    




	Coordinating compliance controls and integrity in the group, ensure implementation and administers, manages controls calendar, system access, less analytical more coordination and data management. Activities: Reports on Business control KPIs, Irregularity logs, Key Activities related to Rep letters, Fixed Asset register and adhoc activities e.g. Awareness training, oi System (oi). Reports to Project Controls Advisor.Works with moderate work direction and is skilled and knowledgeable to the position.


Responsibilities


	Promoting Controls Integrity (CI) & Controls and support to raise staff control awareness
	Identify new processes or changes and ensure all FCPA requirements are properly performed/updated and approved
	Assisting the Business Manager with key activities as required around budgeting and planning activity
	Ensuring controls catalogs are documented/updated and approved as required by Business Manager
	Supports ongoing controls activities (Audits – UIA Planning and coordination, Representation Letter Coordination, Controls Training for staff and maintaining training register, End User Computing Risk Assessments, Access Reviews)
	Update & file Delegation Of Authority Guide (DOAG), FCPA’s, MOC’s, Job Handover checklists
	Responsible for coordinating Management and Protection of Information (MPI) Monthly Inspection & Reporting
	Preparing/coordinating the development of controls documentation
	Prepare monthly oi updates
	Monthly reporting of KPI Scorecards for oi & CI
	Provide awareness of Tools in developing oi System 10-1 for system practitioners
	Oversee the management of the central External Meetings Register and reporting process
	Departmental Records Contact: Serve as Department/Business Unit Subject Matter Expert (SME) and collaborate with the respective Local IMS group
	Distribute Communications about Records Management within the team
	Safeguard the department or business units’ vital records, complete and retain for ready access both physical and electronic records
	Organize and Departmental Shared Drive folders and allocate retention codes according to Records Retention Schedule
	Shared folder structuring/organizing/maintenance, installing and removal of access
	Organize filing/records clean up days – include on Controls Calendar
	Focal point for all documentation and review processes for Departmental owned contracts. Monitors Quality performance and adherence to contract Terms & Conditions
	Administer contracts by monitoring Purchase Order/Agreement end dates and expenditures.
	Raise Service Requisitions for Department/Business Unit’s owned contracts/service providers
	Represents Department/Vendor in contract development with Procurement
	Departmental SharePoint Site Collection Administrator


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Information Management, Business, Economics, or related degree.
	Prior experience in information and record management, administration and data analysis
	Excellent written verbal, communication, presentation skills in English
	Ability to work effectively in a virtual, multi-cultural team environment
	Strong leadership and interpersonal influencing skills
	Self-starter with results and schedule orientation
	Advanced skills in MS Excel, including data validation, charting, pivot tables
	Intermediate skills in Microsoft Office Products including Publisher, Word, PowerPoint
	Knowledge of Microsoft Access desirable.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

2.) Drill Team Quality Representative Coordinator III

Job Location: Lagos

Main Functions


	Assist the Drill Team’s drilling and completion operations by developing and stewarding the Drill Team Quality Plan, including quality assurance (QA) and quality control (QC) functions, throughout planning and execution of well construction activities. Includes oversight of quality for equipment and services provided by Drilling Service Companies and Rig Contractors.


Tasks and Responsibilities
In addition to all Level 1 and 2 tasks and responsibilities, a Level 3 will:


	Be an expert in the field and discipline
	Responsible for data integrity of the quality event database
	Provides training and mentoring to quality team members and drill team engineers


Requirements


	B.Sc. Degree in Engineering or Engineering Technology from a recognized institution and/or previous experience in a closely related position
	Experience in oilfield related work
	Thorough knowledge of sound quality assurance practices. Abreast of best practices and latest technology in meeting minimum industry standards and specifications (such as API Standards, API Spec Q1, API Spec Q2, ISO 9001, American Society for Nondestructive Testing (ASNT), National Association of Corrosion Engineers (NACE), etc.) for manufacturing, inspecting, transporting, installing, maintaining and storing oilfield equipment.
	Knowledge of ASNT inspection practices.
	Knowledge of T.H. Hill DS-1 drill string inspections.
	Completion of ISO 9001 Lead Course.
	Experience and knowledge of oilfield Drilling and Completions Engineering or Operations.
	Experience with Rig Equipment and related operations.
	Good self-motivation and ability to influence others without authority (Drill Team, Quality Team, and Service Providers).
	Very good organizational skills (programs, priorities, sharing/balancing the load).
	Good business writing and computer software literate.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Material Lead III

Job Location: Lagos

Main Functions


	The Materials Lead manages and expedites flow of materials, equipment, parts, and assemblies between internal and external departments and sites, according to delivery and shipping schedules and project priorities, and compiles and maintains computerized records.


Tasks and Responsibilities
In addition to all Level 1 and 2 tasks and responsibilities, Level 3:


	Oversees and ensures the compilation and maintenance manual or computerized records, such as material inventory, delivery, shipping and storage reports, and status and location of materials.
	Monitor and oversee the transport of materials from one department to another.
	Manages and ensures arrangement for repair and/or assembly of company-provided materials, equipment or parts.
	Any additional activities as directed by the Project Manager and Site Manager required to support delivery.


Requirements


	Bachelors’ or Master’s degree required
	Experience in materials management preferred
	General knowledge of industry equipment and materials with ability to positively identify components.
	Extensive knowledge of SAP material catalogs and search tools
	Extensive knowledge of how and where materials are used in the plant
	Knowledgeable of quality control systems and procedures.
	Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Word, Excel, Outlook and Access or equivalent programs.
	Work experience in Oil and Gas industry
	Advanced time management skills
	Excellent problem solving skills
	Advanced written and oral communications skills
	Influencing with and without authority
	Organization skills


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "B2B Sales Manager",
  "description": "Wandel International (Nig) Limited, the flagship company of the Simba Group which started operations in 1988, is a conglomerate having a Pan-Nigeria presence and business interests in some ofthe most dynamic sectors in the economy. The Group is a dominant player in the transportation, powerand IT-enabled service sectors, which are critical to the sustained growth of the Nigerian Economy. The corporate vision of the Group is to provide innovative and quality products along with excellent after-sales service to our customers. The activities of the Group are guided by strong value systems built around Integrity, Innovation, Openness and Consistency.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: B2B Sales Manager

Job Location: Motorized Transport Division (MTD) &ndash; ACME, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reporting line: PCH Institutional Sales
Direct Reports: Sales executives/ Manager

Job Summary


	Responsible for driving and strategic Government, Institutional &amp; Corporate Sales. lead our B2B and public sector sales initiatives in Nigeria&#39;s dynamic automotive industry.
	This role requires deep market knowledge, strong networks within government and corporate sectors, and a consultative sales approach to drive fleet and bulk vehicle sales across key segments.


Key Roles / Responsibilities
Strategic Sales &amp; Market Development:


	Develop and implement strategic sales plans targeting government parastatals, MDAs, NGOs, multinationals, and large Nigerian corporations.
	Monitor and respond to tender opportunities, bid processes, and public procurement frameworks.
	Develop a structured pipeline and close high-value bulk vehicle and fleet supply deals.


Client Relationship Management:


	Cultivate relationships with senior stakeholders in government (federal, state, and local), fleet buyers, and procurement managers in private sector institutions.
	Act as primary point of contact for key clients, ensuring high service standards and after-sales engagement.
	Organize product demonstrations, test-drive events, and tailored presentations for key decision-makers.


Team &amp; Cross-functional Coordination:


	Work with internal stakeholders (logistics, finance, legal, technical, and service teams) to structure deals, resolve challenges, and ensure seamless delivery.
	Lead or support a small B2B/government sales team to ensure goal alignment and performance.


Reporting &amp; Compliance:


	Maintain accurate records of all client activities using CRM tools.
	Provide weekly/monthly reports on sales pipelines, forecasts, and market insights.
	Ensure strict compliance with Nigerian procurement laws, anti-corruption policies, and internal company ethics.


KPIs / Success Metrics


	Monthly &amp; Quarterly Sales Target Achievement (%)
	Average Deal Size (?/contract)
	New Institutional/Government Accounts Opened (monthly/quarterly)
	Sales Pipeline Conversion Rate (%)
	Client Retention Rate (%) - Repeat purchase rate or renewal contracts.
	Sales Forecast Accuracy (%)
	Compliance with Public Procurement Protocols (%)
	Partnerships or MOUs Signed with Institutions
	Number of B2B Events or Product Demos Organized

B2B Sales Manager Job at Wandel International (Nig) Limited




        
    

        
        Wandel International (Nig) Limited, the flagship company of the Simba Group which started operations in 1988, is a conglomerate having a Pan-Nigeria presence and business interests in some ofthe most dynamic sectors in the economy. The Group is a dominant player in the transportation, powerand IT-enabled service sectors, which are critical to the sustained growth of the Nigerian Economy. The corporate vision of the Group is to provide innovative and quality products along with excellent after-sales service to our customers. The activities of the Group are guided by strong value systems built around Integrity, Innovation, Openness and Consistency.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: B2B Sales Manager
     

            
    



Job Location: Motorized Transport Division (MTD) – ACME, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Reporting line: PCH Institutional Sales
Direct Reports: Sales executives/ Manager

Job Summary


	Responsible for driving and strategic Government, Institutional & Corporate Sales. lead our B2B and public sector sales initiatives in Nigeria's dynamic automotive industry.
	This role requires deep market knowledge, strong networks within government and corporate sectors, and a consultative sales approach to drive fleet and bulk vehicle sales across key segments.


Key Roles / Responsibilities
Strategic Sales & Market Development:


	Develop and implement strategic sales plans targeting government parastatals, MDAs, NGOs, multinationals, and large Nigerian corporations.
	Monitor and respond to tender opportunities, bid processes, and public procurement frameworks.
	Develop a structured pipeline and close high-value bulk vehicle and fleet supply deals.


Client Relationship Management:


	Cultivate relationships with senior stakeholders in government (federal, state, and local), fleet buyers, and procurement managers in private sector institutions.
	Act as primary point of contact for key clients, ensuring high service standards and after-sales engagement.
	Organize product demonstrations, test-drive events, and tailored presentations for key decision-makers.


Team & Cross-functional Coordination:


	Work with internal stakeholders (logistics, finance, legal, technical, and service teams) to structure deals, resolve challenges, and ensure seamless delivery.
	Lead or support a small B2B/government sales team to ensure goal alignment and performance.


Reporting & Compliance:


	Maintain accurate records of all client activities using CRM tools.
	Provide weekly/monthly reports on sales pipelines, forecasts, and market insights.
	Ensure strict compliance with Nigerian procurement laws, anti-corruption policies, and internal company ethics.


KPIs / Success Metrics


	Monthly & Quarterly Sales Target Achievement (%)
	Average Deal Size (?/contract)
	New Institutional/Government Accounts Opened (monthly/quarterly)
	Sales Pipeline Conversion Rate (%)
	Client Retention Rate (%) - Repeat purchase rate or renewal contracts.
	Sales Forecast Accuracy (%)
	Compliance with Public Procurement Protocols (%)
	Partnerships or MOUs Signed with Institutions
	Number of B2B Events or Product Demos Organized
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Control Manager (Automobile)",
  "description": "Wandel International Limited, a Flagship Company of Simba Group, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Manager (Automobile)

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Base: Motorized Transport Division (MTD) &ndash; ACME, Lagos
Reporting line: MTD Assembly
Direct Reports: Assembly Head

Job Description


	To ensure that all 2 &amp; 3-wheeler vehicles assembled meet defined quality standards and specifications by implementing effective quality control processes across the assembly line, minimizing defects, and ensuring compliance with safety and regulatory requirements.


Key Roles / Responsibilities
Quality Leadership &amp; Strategy:


	Develop, implement, and continuously improve quality control strategies, policies, and procedures for vehicle assembly operations.
	Lead and supervise the Quality Control team to ensure compliance with established quality standards.
	Establish quality objectives, monitor performance, and drive continuous improvement initiatives.
	Promote a quality-first culture across the production floor.


Assembly Line Quality Control:


	Oversee in-process inspections at all critical assembly stages including chassis assembly, engine installation, electrical wiring, body assembly, painting, and final finishing.
	Ensure strict adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), engineering drawings, and work instructions.
	Verify torque settings, alignments, clearances, and component fitments according to specifications.
	Ensure process discipline and standardization throughout the assembly line.


Final Vehicle Inspection (PDI):


	Manage Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) activities before vehicle dispatch.
	Verify engine performance, braking systems, electrical systems, suspension, steering, fit &amp; finish, lighting, and overall build quality.
	Approve vehicles for release or hold units requiring corrective action.
	Ensure zero defective vehicles leave the factory.


Defect Management &amp; Root Cause Analysis:


	Lead investigations into quality defects and non-conformities.
	Conduct Root Cause Analysis (RCA) using methodologies such as:
	
		5 Whys
		Fishbone Diagram
		Pareto Analysis
		8D Problem Solving
	
	
	Drive Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPA).
	Monitor recurring issues and ensure permanent corrective actions are implemented.


Process Quality Management:


	Monitor manufacturing processes to ensure consistent quality output.
	Collaborate with Production, Engineering, Maintenance, and Procurement to resolve quality issues.
	Drive First Time Right (FTR) manufacturing practices.
	Introduce process improvements that reduce variation and improve product quality.


Incoming Material &amp; Supplier Quality:


	Oversee inspection of CKD/SKD kits, components, and raw materials.
	Ensure defective or non-conforming materials are quarantined and reported.
	Work with Procurement and suppliers to resolve quality concerns.
	Monitor supplier quality performance and recommend improvement actions.


Quality Systems, Documentation &amp; Reporting:


	Establish and maintain inspection records, quality reports, defect logs, and traceability documentation.
	Develop quality dashboards and performance reports for management.
	Monitor key quality metrics including:
	
		First Pass Yield (FPY)
		First Time Right (FTR)
		Rework Rate
		Defect Per Vehicle (DPV)
		Customer Complaints
		Warranty Claims
	
	
	Present quality performance during management review meetings.


Compliance &amp; Quality Audits:


	Ensure compliance with internal quality standards and automotive manufacturing requirements.
	Lead internal quality audits and support external audits.
	Ensure audit findings are addressed through effective corrective action plans.
	Maintain documentation required for regulatory compliance and quality certifications.


Continuous Improvement:


	Lead Lean Manufacturing, Kaizen, and Six Sigma improvement initiatives.
	Identify opportunities to reduce waste, improve productivity, and enhance product quality.
	Recommend improvements to production processes, tooling, fixtures, and inspection methods.
	Support new product introduction (NPI) and process validation activities.


Training &amp; Team Development:


	Train Quality Officers, Inspectors, and Assembly personnel on quality standards and inspection procedures.
	Build team capability in problem-solving and quality tools.
	Promote a Zero Defect and Right First Time culture.
	Conduct periodic quality awareness sessions across the assembly department.


Qualification and Experience


	HND / Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Mechanical, Automotive, or Industrial Engineering
	5 - 10 years of experience in automobile assembly or manufacturing (3-wheeler/2-wheeler preferred) with 2-3 years in supervisory role.
	Familiarity with CKD/SKD assembly processes is an advantage.


Skills and Competencies:


	Strong understanding of automotive assembly systems( engine, transmission, electricals)
	Knowledge of quality tools (RCA, 5 Whys, Fishbone, Pareto)
	Attention to detail and inspection accuracy
	Problem-solving and analytical thinking
	Good communication and reporting skills
	Basic knowledge of MS Excel and reporting tools.


Working Conditions:


	Shop floor / assembly line environment
	Requires standing, walking, and active supervision
	May require shift work depending on production schedule.

Quality Control Manager (Automobile) Job at Wandel International Limited




        
    

        
        Wandel International Limited, a Flagship Company of Simba Group, is recruiting suitable candidates to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Control Manager (Automobile)

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Base: Motorized Transport Division (MTD) – ACME, Lagos
Reporting line: MTD Assembly
Direct Reports: Assembly Head

Job Description
     

            
    




	To ensure that all 2 & 3-wheeler vehicles assembled meet defined quality standards and specifications by implementing effective quality control processes across the assembly line, minimizing defects, and ensuring compliance with safety and regulatory requirements.


Key Roles / Responsibilities
Quality Leadership & Strategy:


	Develop, implement, and continuously improve quality control strategies, policies, and procedures for vehicle assembly operations.
	Lead and supervise the Quality Control team to ensure compliance with established quality standards.
	Establish quality objectives, monitor performance, and drive continuous improvement initiatives.
	Promote a quality-first culture across the production floor.


Assembly Line Quality Control:


	Oversee in-process inspections at all critical assembly stages including chassis assembly, engine installation, electrical wiring, body assembly, painting, and final finishing.
	Ensure strict adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), engineering drawings, and work instructions.
	Verify torque settings, alignments, clearances, and component fitments according to specifications.
	Ensure process discipline and standardization throughout the assembly line.


Final Vehicle Inspection (PDI):


	Manage Pre-Delivery Inspection (PDI) activities before vehicle dispatch.
	Verify engine performance, braking systems, electrical systems, suspension, steering, fit & finish, lighting, and overall build quality.
	Approve vehicles for release or hold units requiring corrective action.
	Ensure zero defective vehicles leave the factory.


Defect Management & Root Cause Analysis:


	Lead investigations into quality defects and non-conformities.
	Conduct Root Cause Analysis (RCA) using methodologies such as:
	
		5 Whys
		Fishbone Diagram
		Pareto Analysis
		8D Problem Solving
	
	
	Drive Corrective and Preventive Actions (CAPA).
	Monitor recurring issues and ensure permanent corrective actions are implemented.


Process Quality Management:


	Monitor manufacturing processes to ensure consistent quality output.
	Collaborate with Production, Engineering, Maintenance, and Procurement to resolve quality issues.
	Drive First Time Right (FTR) manufacturing practices.
	Introduce process improvements that reduce variation and improve product quality.


Incoming Material & Supplier Quality:


	Oversee inspection of CKD/SKD kits, components, and raw materials.
	Ensure defective or non-conforming materials are quarantined and reported.
	Work with Procurement and suppliers to resolve quality concerns.
	Monitor supplier quality performance and recommend improvement actions.


Quality Systems, Documentation & Reporting:


	Establish and maintain inspection records, quality reports, defect logs, and traceability documentation.
	Develop quality dashboards and performance reports for management.
	Monitor key quality metrics including:
	
		First Pass Yield (FPY)
		First Time Right (FTR)
		Rework Rate
		Defect Per Vehicle (DPV)
		Customer Complaints
		Warranty Claims
	
	
	Present quality performance during management review meetings.


Compliance & Quality Audits:


	Ensure compliance with internal quality standards and automotive manufacturing requirements.
	Lead internal quality audits and support external audits.
	Ensure audit findings are addressed through effective corrective action plans.
	Maintain documentation required for regulatory compliance and quality certifications.


Continuous Improvement:


	Lead Lean Manufacturing, Kaizen, and Six Sigma improvement initiatives.
	Identify opportunities to reduce waste, improve productivity, and enhance product quality.
	Recommend improvements to production processes, tooling, fixtures, and inspection methods.
	Support new product introduction (NPI) and process validation activities.


Training & Team Development:


	Train Quality Officers, Inspectors, and Assembly personnel on quality standards and inspection procedures.
	Build team capability in problem-solving and quality tools.
	Promote a Zero Defect and Right First Time culture.
	Conduct periodic quality awareness sessions across the assembly department.


Qualification and Experience


	HND / Bachelor’s Degree in Mechanical, Automotive, or Industrial Engineering
	5 - 10 years of experience in automobile assembly or manufacturing (3-wheeler/2-wheeler preferred) with 2-3 years in supervisory role.
	Familiarity with CKD/SKD assembly processes is an advantage.


Skills and Competencies:


	Strong understanding of automotive assembly systems( engine, transmission, electricals)
	Knowledge of quality tools (RCA, 5 Whys, Fishbone, Pareto)
	Attention to detail and inspection accuracy
	Problem-solving and analytical thinking
	Good communication and reporting skills
	Basic knowledge of MS Excel and reporting tools.


Working Conditions:


	Shop floor / assembly line environment
	Requires standing, walking, and active supervision
	May require shift work depending on production schedule.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Bank Accountant",
  "description": "MarbleClear Limited, a human Resource Consulting firm, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bank Accountant

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are looking for a detail-oriented and analytical Bank Accountant to join our Finance team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing banking transactions, performing bank reconciliations, monitoring loan and foreign exchange activities, and ensuring the accuracy of banking records while maintaining compliance with internal controls and financial regulations.


Key Responsibilities


	Review and record daily banking transactions, including deposits, withdrawals, transfers, and journal entries.
	Perform timely and accurate bank reconciliations, ensuring all discrepancies are investigated and resolved.
	Monitor bank charges, commissions, and interest to ensure they align with agreed banking terms and contracts.
	Prepare documentation for bank credit facilities, including loans, letters of credit, and letters of guarantee, and follow up on approvals and utilization.
	Monitor long-term loan balances, repayment schedules, accrued interest, and prepare related accounting entries.
	Initiate and register electronic Form M for import transactions on the Nigeria Single Window Trade Portal.
	Revalue foreign currency Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable (AR) balances at month-end.
	Calculate and post unrealized foreign exchange gains and losses in the General Ledger.
	Ensure compliance with banking regulations, company policies, and internal control procedures.
	Support month-end closing activities and other finance-related assignments as required.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline.
	2&ndash;3 years of relevant accounting experience, preferably in an FMCG, manufacturing, or similar environment.
	Strong understanding of accounting principles and bank reconciliation processes.
	Experience managing loans, foreign exchange transactions, and banking operations is an added advantage.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and SAP ERP.
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
	High level of accuracy, integrity, and attention to detail.
	Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.


Salary
N350,000 - N400,000 / month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qulified candidates should send their CV to: career.precendia@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "MarbleClear"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Oyo",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },

    "value": "202168"

Bank Accountant Job at MarbleClear Limited




        
    

        
        MarbleClear Limited, a human Resource Consulting firm, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Bank Accountant

Job Location: Ibadan, Oyo
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are looking for a detail-oriented and analytical Bank Accountant to join our Finance team.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for managing banking transactions, performing bank reconciliations, monitoring loan and foreign exchange activities, and ensuring the accuracy of banking records while maintaining compliance with internal controls and financial regulations.


Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Review and record daily banking transactions, including deposits, withdrawals, transfers, and journal entries.
	Perform timely and accurate bank reconciliations, ensuring all discrepancies are investigated and resolved.
	Monitor bank charges, commissions, and interest to ensure they align with agreed banking terms and contracts.
	Prepare documentation for bank credit facilities, including loans, letters of credit, and letters of guarantee, and follow up on approvals and utilization.
	Monitor long-term loan balances, repayment schedules, accrued interest, and prepare related accounting entries.
	Initiate and register electronic Form M for import transactions on the Nigeria Single Window Trade Portal.
	Revalue foreign currency Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable (AR) balances at month-end.
	Calculate and post unrealized foreign exchange gains and losses in the General Ledger.
	Ensure compliance with banking regulations, company policies, and internal control procedures.
	Support month-end closing activities and other finance-related assignments as required.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline.
	2–3 years of relevant accounting experience, preferably in an FMCG, manufacturing, or similar environment.
	Strong understanding of accounting principles and bank reconciliation processes.
	Experience managing loans, foreign exchange transactions, and banking operations is an added advantage.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and SAP ERP.
	Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
	High level of accuracy, integrity, and attention to detail.
	Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.


Salary
N350,000 - N400,000 / month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qulified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
